SHTM AI Challenge Studio

Where Industry Challenges Meet AI-Powered Student Innovation

Real problems. AI solutions. Real impact.
We connect industry, faculty, alumni and students to solve authentic challenges with AI, turning learning into measurable outcomes.

Explore ChallengesHow It Works

Talent PathwaysFrom learning to PoC and careers.
Studio Principle
In the age of AI, we do not just learn AI. We use AI to solve real-world problems.

SHTM AI Challenge Studio connects authentic industry challenges, AI-enabled student innovation, and talent development through a scalable challenge-based learning mechanism.

Real Problems. AI Solutions. Real Impact.

Our Approach

From Challenge to Impact

A structured journey that turns real-world problems into AI-enabled learning, prototypes, and collaboration opportunities.

Industry Challenge

Partners and alumni submit real business challenges.

Engage & Curate

Faculty refine and scope challenges for impact and feasibility.

Student Innovation

Students form teams and apply AI to build solutions.

Build & Develop

Prototypes are developed with mentorship and support.

Demo Day

Teams pitch to industry and expert judges.

AI-Driven Impact

Winning solutions drive PoC, internships, or collaboration.

Challenge Bank

Real Problems. Real Impact.

Explore challenge categories across hospitality and tourism. Each challenge is designed to be authentic, teachable, and AI-actionable.

GuestAI-actionable

Guest Experience & Personalisation

How might a hotel use AI to personalise pre-arrival, in-stay and post-stay communication without losing brand voice?

Hospitality partnerService designMVP feasible
RevenueAI-actionable

Revenue & Demand Optimisation

How can AI help a small hotel forecast demand signals and recommend practical pricing actions for non-technical managers?

Hotel groupAnalyticsIndustry data
OperationsAI-actionable

Operations & Automation

How might an AI workflow reduce repetitive front-office tasks while preserving service warmth and accountability?

Operations teamWorkflowAgent prototype
SustainabilityAI-actionable

Sustainability & ESG

How can a tourism business turn fragmented sustainability data into guest-facing stories and internal action dashboards?

Destination partnerESGStory + dashboard
GuestAI-actionable

Marketing & Growth

How can AI help translate guest feedback and social content into campaign ideas for niche travel segments?

Alumni founderMarketingCreative AI
TalentAI-actionable

Talent & Learning

How can a hospitality employer identify students with AI problem-solving capability through project portfolios instead of CVs alone?

HR partnerTalentPortfolio-based
Student AI Studio

AI-Enabled Prototyping

Students learn AI by using it to frame problems, build prototypes, test ideas, and communicate solutions in authentic industry contexts.

No-Code First

Students build AI solutions using accessible, practical and fast tools.

AI-Native

Generative AI is integrated into problem framing, research, design and prototyping.

Dual Mentorship

Each team receives guidance from academic and industry mentors.

Career Advantage

Students develop evidence of capability through real project portfolios.

Turn messy business problems into AI-actionable briefs.

Students clarify users, pain points, data sources, constraints, and success criteria before building.

Challenge brief: problem context, target user, expected outcome, resources, and prototype boundary.

Use AI tools to create MVPs, workflows, and service concepts.

The emphasis is not coding for its own sake, but using AI to move from diagnosis to workable solutions.

Prototype examples: chatbot, dashboard, agent workflow, content engine, service recovery assistant, or training tool.

Present solutions to real industry reviewers.

External evaluation creates pathways for PoC, internship, and talent recognition.

Demo Day evaluation: relevance, feasibility, user value, AI use, implementation potential, and team capability.
Pilot Scope

Pilot Targets

Conservative first-cycle goals for validating the mechanism before wider school-level scaling.

10+Challenge Briefs Curated
5+Industry & Alumni Partners
1–2Course Pilots
40+Student Participants
8+AI MVPs Developed

Academic Edge

  • Faculty expertise
  • Research-backed approach
  • Rigorous evaluation standards

Market Relevance

  • Real business challenges
  • Practical AI solutions
  • Measurable outcomes
